domain: Welsh penillion cerdd dant (Welsh 'string music'; solo singer improvises counter-melody against fixed harp melody; bardic duel-like performance)
specific_mechanism: 2-agent fixed-vs-improvised counterpoint where Agent A emits primary answer following fixed policy, Agent B emits a non-redundant counter-melody enforced by KL-divergence ≥ε against A's logits, third aggregator produces harmonic resolution.
llm_application: PENILLION-COUNTER: a 2-agent fixed-vs-improvised LLM dialogue protocol. Agent A (harp) is FIXED — runs a deterministic policy/canonical answer. Agent B (penillion) ATTENDS to A's token stream and emits a counter-melody answer constrained by a non-redundancy regularizer: KL(B||A) ≥ ε at every position. A third aggregator Agent C reconciles A and B into a harmonic-resolution output via weighted ensembling. Differs from Multi-Agent Debate (which seeks consensus), RECONCILE (weighted vote of independent agents), Adversarial Debate (zero-sum) by combining (a) fixed-improvised asymmetry, (b) explicit non-redundancy KL constraint, (c) harmonic-resolution aggregator.
novelty_claim: Multi-Agent Debate, RECONCILE, Adversarial Debate, Counterpoint Music Generation already exist. PENILLION-COUNTER recombines fixed-policy A + KL-constrained improviser B + aggregator C. Small extension of existing components; not a wholly new artefact.
